response.justification: The candidate is a capable, versatile engineer, but he misses every mandatory pillar of this role. His C++ experience amounts to a seven-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 plus a thesis project, nowhere near the required 5-10 years of hands-on Windows C++. His database background is MySQL and MongoDB rather than SQL Server or Oracle, and he has no fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking exposure — RIDE Capital was a Node/TypeScript fintech role, not capital markets systems work. His recent decade has been web and full-stack work in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, which does not transfer to this consulting engagement's core demands.
strengths: Broad, polyglot engineering background spanning web, desktop, and low-latency backend systems
Excellent communication skills, demonstrated through conference talks, workshops, and teaching
Solid engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and DevOps tooling
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated (~7 months professionally, over a decade ago), versus the mandatory 5-10 years on Windows
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database work is limited to MySQL and MongoDB
No fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking domain knowledge

key_factors.factor: No capital markets or fixed income domain exposure
Strong general engineering and communication skills
